text.title: chunking-code-ast Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for chunking-code-ast as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the JS regex-based path for TypeScript files with decorators, generics, or complex arrow functions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For multi-language codebases, prefer tree-sitter bindings over regex-based JS chunking — the regex patterns in the reference implementation fail on arrow functions with implicit returns, TypeScript generics, and decorator syntax. Install tree-sitter for production TypeScript RAG.

failure-mode.description: Indexing CodeChunker output from Python files without deduplicating overlapping method/class chunks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CodeChunker via AST produces overlapping chunks when a class contains methods — both the class body and each method appear as separate chunks. Deduplicate by (name, start_line) before indexing to avoid duplicate vector entries that skew nearest-neighbor scores.
